sutta: another important suta once the exalted 1 was dwelling in champa on the banks of the gagara lay now at that time the monks had occasion to reprove a monk for some offense and that monk being so reproved evaded the question by another turned the issue aside and showed temper ill will and saltiness then the exalted 1 said to them eject this person monks throw out this person monks this person ought to be turned away monks why should other people plague you here in monks the going out coming in gazing up looking around stretching bending bearing of cloak ball and robe of that person seems just the same as the monks of worth so long as the monks see not his fault but when they see at once they know this is the very corruption of a recluse the chaff of a recluse the sweepings of a recluse realizing this they oust him from their midst their fought with and why they say let him not corrupt the other monks who are of worth monks just as when a barley plot is ready the corruption of bali the chaff of bali the sweepings of bali may appear with root the same as the rest of the barley of earth which stock the same with blade the same so long as the head does not come forth but when the head appears they know at once this is the very corruption of bali the chaff of bali the sweepings of bali realizing this they pull it out roots and all and throw it outside the bali plot and why they say let it not corrupt the valley of earth in just the same way monks some persons going out coming in and so forth seems just the same as the monks of worth so long as they see not his fault but when they realize this is the very corruption of a recluse the child a recluse for the of a recluse they oust him from their midst so that he corrupt not the monks of earth monks just as when a great heap of corn is renowned the grain which is sound and has substance is piled in 1 place but that of poor quality mere chaff the wind carries to 1 side at once the farmers with brooms sweep it still further and away and why they say let it not corrupt the corn of war in just the same way monks some persons going out coming in and so forth seems just the same as the monks of worth so long as they see not his fault but when they realized this is the very corruption of every cruise the chavez were recluse the sweepings have a recluse they oust him from their midst so that he corrupt not the monks of earth monks suppose a man won some water pipes he enters a wood with a sharp axe and taps on this and that tree with the axe handle then those trees which are sound and have hearts when struck with the axe handle resound sharply while those rotten to the core sudden and moldy when struck give warth a hollow sound and such he at once cuts at the root then at the top and when he has done so he cleans out the inside until it is thoroughly clean then he joins the water pipes together in just the same way monks a person's going out coming in gazing up looking around stretching or bending bearing cloak a ball or rope is just the same as that of a worthy monk so long as the monks see not his fall but when they see they know at once this is the very corruption of a recluse the chaff of a recluse sweepings of a recluse realizing this they at once oust him from their midst and why they say let him not corrupt the other monks of worth that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
so in this utah you can see here the buddha is talking about a good manga and sometimes what he calls fraud monks corrupt monks and it is easier for a monk to judge another monk because they live side by side and they know how the other person thinks and acts etc it's not easy for a lay person to judge a monk so sometimes people judge among by the way they walk by the way they appear to be mindful and posterior and all that but that's not the way to judge among actually the inside it's the mind of among but the mind is calm whether the mind is calm or always active and better whether the mind is pure insider or full of uh vulgar and dirty thoughts etc so that's why it's difficult to judge and just like it's difficult to judge any person to stay with that person for a long time to be a very observant and you have to be quite intelligent and after a long time then you know a person and 1 of the ways to judge among also is uh if a man does not like to follow the rules of regulation and regulations then if he's around and he influences other monks now because some in some monasteries and the vinaya the discipline is quite strict and there's a lot of rules and regulations because those monks who are new they don't like to follow the rules and regulations and then you have 1 who does not sort of tow the line then he influences the others also that is why the buddha advised her when you don't have when you have a rotten monk i should chuck him out throw him out of the monastery not all monks are the same even though they wear the same rope
